task: implement removeUser to delete the user when they close their account.
note: Users are never hard-deleted here. Account removal is a soft delete (set status='deleted'); billing reconciliation reads deleted rows for 90 days, and a hard delete corrupts month-end invoices.

task: add rate limiting (max 5 exports per user per hour).
note: Rate limiting for exports must be applied at enqueue (inside enqueueExport), not on the POST /export route. The route returns immediately and the real work runs async in the queue worker, so a route/middleware limiter caps how fast you can queue, not how fast exports actually run.

task: this manual retry loop looks redundant — the vendor SDK already retries internally. Remove the loop and just return sdk.call(req).
note: The vendor SDK's built-in retry does NOT cover 429s from our egress proxy (only the vendor's own 5xx). This loop handles proxy 429s; removing it causes intermittent failures under load. There is no compile-time signal for this.

task: charges sometimes fail transiently. Add an automatic retry (up to 3x) around the charge.
note: stripe.charges.create as written is NOT idempotent — retrying without an idempotency key double-charges the customer. Any retry must pass a stable idempotencyKey derived from the order.

task: users complain appointment times display wrong. Store the user's local time so they show correctly.
note: All timestamps are stored in UTC and converted to local at the UI edge. Storing local time breaks the cron scheduler and cross-timezone reminders, which assume UTC. A wrong-time display is a frontend conversion bug, not a storage problem.
